ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT TEGSCO LLC, D.B.A SAN FRANCISCO AUTORETURN (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AUTO-RETURN")

LAW AND MOTION, 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT BASED IN OFFICIAL DOCUMENTS AND DEFENDANT ADMISSIONS PROVING CITY RACKETEERING TO CRIMINAL STANDARDS - THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER BECAUSE: (1) THE NOTICE OF MOTION IS DEFECTIVE AND DOES NOT SET FORTH THE GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION (CCP 1010); (2) PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED FACTS (CCP 437C(B)(1); CRC 3.1350(D); AND (3) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SUBMIT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING THAT HE CAN ESTABLISH HIS CLAIMS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EHG

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Off Calendar

Request For Judicial Notice And Declaration Re How This Corrupt Court Is Violating Its Own Local Rule 8.2 By Illegally Withholding An As Of Right Motion Date (Motion For Reconsideration)
Off Calendar Jan-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S Motion For Reconsideration of 10/26/15 Order Re: Summary Judgment is off calendar. Notice of Removal filed on December 10, 2015.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2015 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-02-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Based In Official Documents And Defendant Admissions Proving City Racketeering To Criminal Standards
Off Calendar Oct-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - PATRICK MISSUD, TEL. NO. 1-415-584-7251, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. SCOTT EMBLIDGE OF MOSCONE EMLIDGE & OTIS LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-362-3599 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDNAT TEGSCO.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF PATRICK MISSUD'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Based In Official Documents And Defendant Admissions Proving City Racketeering To Criminal Standards - The motion is denied. The motion is procedurally improper because: (1) the notice of motion is defective and does not set forth the grounds for the motion (CCP 1010); (2) plaintiff failed to provide a separate statement of undisputed facts (CCP 437c(b)(1); CRC 3.1350(d); and (3) plaintiff does not submit admissible evidence demonstrating that he can establish his claims. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2015 Continued To Dec-16-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

REQUEST PENDING TO WAIVE COURT FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO G.C. 68633, CRC 3.51, 8.26, AND 8.818
Off Calendar Jul-15-2015 Denied
